extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won a chaotic sprint after teammate DILLIER Silvan collected 3 bonus seconds at the intermediate sprint positioning himself second overall.
tactical_patterns: DILLIER Silvan prioritized intermediate sprint bonus seconds finishing second overall, revealing LottoNL-Jumbo's dual GC-sprint stage strategy
Quick-Step deployed late disruption attacks inside 10km not for breakaway success but to position JAKOBSEN Fabio optimally
Peloton never allowed early break beyond one minute gap despite short 154km stage, indicating sprint teams feared bonus seconds
TRENTIN Matteo's Mitchelton-Scott led out too early as they peeled off before final 500m allowing GROENEWEGEN clear run

rider_signals.DILLIER Silvan: positive: Aggressively targeted intermediate sprint bonus seconds, suggesting GC ambitions beyond traditional sprint lead-out role
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: Fourteenth win of season demonstrates sustained peak form late in World Tour calendar
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: neutral: Strong second place but couldn't match GROENEWEGEN in final 200m despite good positioning
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: neutral: Quick-Step tactics got him positioned well but couldn't convert in maiden World Tour sprint finish
rider_signals.HALLER Marco: positive: After six-month broken kneecap layoff, attacked for intermediate sprint showing recovered race intensity tolerance
rider_signals.KENNAUGH Peter: neutral: Scored KOM points on final climb indicating Bora positioning for multiple classification opportunities
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: negative: Led out too early allowing GROENEWEGEN to benefit from Mitchelton-Scott's pace setting
